title: Reduced Motion Toggle

statement.markdown:
## Reduced Motion Toggle

Create a reduced motion toggle that allows users to choose whether to enable or disable reduced motion effects on a website.

### Requirements
1. The toggle button should be displayed on every page.
2. When the user clicks the toggle button, it should store the user's preference in local storage.
3. The website should reduce motion effects based on the user's preference. For example, when the user scrolls, the website should use a reduced motion effect.

constraints: The solution should only use JavaScript, HTML, and CSS.
The solution should use Tailwind CSS classes for styling.
The reduced motion effect should be applied to the entire website, not just individual elements.
The solution should handle cases where the user's browser does not support local storage.

starterCode.html:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <title>Reduced Motion Toggle</title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css">
</head>
<body>
    <button id="reduced-motion-toggle" class="px-4 py-2 border border-gray-400 rounded-lg">Reduced Motion Toggle</button>
    <script src="script.js"></script>
</body>
</html>

gradingCriteria.description: The toggle button is displayed on every page.
The user's preference is stored in local storage.
The reduced motion effect is applied to the entire website.
The solution handles cases where the user's browser does not support local storage.
